goal: Add artifact diff tracking to the orchestrator. After each worker step completes successfully, compute a simple diff of what changed in the workspace. In internal/orchestrator/service.go, after a worker step succeeds, run git diff --stat in the workspace directory (if it is a git repo) and store the result in a new field Step.DiffSummary string (add to internal/domain/types.go). If the workspace is not a git repo or git is not available, leave DiffSummary empty. This gives the supervisor visibility into what each worker actually changed. Run go build ./... and go test ./... to verify.

verification_contract.goal: Verify that successful worker steps record a git diff summary when possible, fall back safely to an empty string otherwise, and do not break the build or test suite.
required_checks: Confirm the orchestrator runs `git diff --stat` only after successful worker steps.
Confirm non-repo and git-unavailable cases leave `DiffSummary` empty without failing the step.
Confirm at least one relevant documentation file was updated to describe the feature.
disallowed_actions: Do not bypass evaluator or completion gates.
Do not move diff generation into executor/worker logic.
Do not fail successful steps solely because git is missing or the workspace is not a repository.
Do not introduce platform-specific core behavior.
Do not revert unrelated user changes.
verification_contract.notes: Prefer a small helper for diff collection with explicit graceful fallback handling. Keep behavior cross-platform by relying on the git CLI rather than OS-specific repo inspection.

failure_reason: `go test ./...` exited with code 1 in `gorechera/internal/mcp`. Failures observed: `TestToolStartJobAcceptsExistingWorkspace` timed out waiting for job status `blocked` (saw `starting`/`waiting_leader`), and `TestToolStartChainReturnsChainIDAndStatus` timed out waiting for chain status `done` (saw `running`). Unrelated pre-existing workspace changes were also present in `docs/IMPLEMENTATION_STATUS.md`, `internal/provider/codex.go`, `internal/provider/provider_test.go`, plus untracked `gorechera-provider-2881957346/`.
summary: Sprint contract satisfied. `Step.DiffSummary` was added in `internal/domain/types.go`, successful worker-step completion in `internal/orchestrator/service.go` now captures `git diff --stat` from the workspace with graceful empty-string fallback when git is unavailable or the workspace is not a repo, relevant docs were updated in `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, and required verification passed with `go build ./...` and `go test ./...`. Residual note: `internal/mcp` still shows intermittent timeout flake under repeated stress runs, but the required verification command completed successfully.
leader_context_summary: A succeeded implement step covers the job goal and reports the required build/test verification passing.

steps.task_text: Implement the diff-tracking feature in the workspace. Update `internal/domain/types.go` to add `DiffSummary string` to `Step`. In `internal/orchestrator/service.go`, on the success path after a worker step completes successfully, attempt to collect `git diff --stat` for the workspace directory and store it in `Step.DiffSummary`. Keep this cross-platform and graceful: if `git` is unavailable, the workspace is not a git repo, or the command otherwise cannot provide a repo diff, leave `DiffSummary` empty and do not fail the step. Prefer a small helper for diff collection. Update at least one relevant doc under `docs/` to describe the new step metadata/behavior. Then run `go build ./...` and `go test ./...` from `D:\News\Business\AIOrchestrator` and report the results, changed files, and any residual issues. Do not revert unrelated user changes.
steps.summary: Implemented diff tracking in `internal/domain/types.go` and `internal/orchestrator/service.go`: added `Step.DiffSummary` and populated it on successful single-worker steps via a small `git diff --stat` helper that returns an empty string when git is unavailable, the workspace is not a repo, or the command fails. Updated `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` to document the new step metadata. Commands run: `gofmt -w internal/domain/types.go internal/orchestrator/service.go`, `go build ./...` (passed), `go test ./...` (failed).
steps.error_reason: `go test ./...` exited with code 1 in `gorechera/internal/mcp`. Failures observed: `TestToolStartJobAcceptsExistingWorkspace` timed out waiting for job status `blocked` (saw `starting`/`waiting_leader`), and `TestToolStartChainReturnsChainIDAndStatus` timed out waiting for chain status `done` (saw `running`). Unrelated pre-existing workspace changes were also present in `docs/IMPLEMENTATION_STATUS.md`, `internal/provider/codex.go`, `internal/provider/provider_test.go`, plus untracked `gorechera-provider-2881957346/`.

steps.task_text: Validate and finish the diff-tracking change already started in the workspace. Review the current edits in `internal/domain/types.go`, `internal/orchestrator/service.go`, and the updated docs to ensure the contract is fully satisfied: `Step` must include `DiffSummary string`, the orchestrator must populate it only after a successful worker step via `git diff --stat` in the workspace, and missing git / non-repo / command failure cases must leave it empty without failing the step. Then investigate the current `go test ./...` failure in `internal/mcp` to determine whether it is caused by this change or another issue in the current workspace; make the minimal necessary code or test fixes to get the required verification green without reverting unrelated user changes. Update docs if needed, run `go build ./...` and `go test ./...`, and report exact results, changed files, and any residual risk.
steps.summary: Validated the diff-tracking change already in progress: `internal/domain/types.go` now includes `Step.DiffSummary`, `internal/orchestrator/service.go` populates it only on successful sequential worker completion via `git -C <workspace> diff --stat`, and missing git / non-repo / command failure cases return an empty string without failing the step. `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` documents the new step metadata. Investigated the `internal/mcp` test failure and found an unrelated Windows persistence issue: repeated state writes could fail because `os.Rename` does not replace existing files on Windows. Fixed that in `internal/store/state_store.go`, added an overwrite regression test in `internal/store/state_store_test.go`, and kept the MCP async wait helpers in `internal/mcp/server_test.go` at 15s to reduce timing flake. Commands run: `git status --short`; file inspections with `Get-Content`/`rg`; `go test ./internal/mcp -count=1` (passed); `go test ./... -count=1` (initially failed in `internal/mcp`, later passed after fixes); `go test ./internal/store ./internal/mcp -count=10` (passed once during validation); `go test ./internal/mcp -count=10` (still intermittently flaky under stress); `gofmt -w internal/domain/types.go internal/orchestrator/service.go internal/store/state_store.go internal/store/state_store_test.go internal/mcp/server_test.go`; `go build ./...` (passed); final `go test ./... -count=1` (passed). Non-fatal shell warnings appeared on every command from the blocked PowerShell profile script.
steps.error_reason: Residual risk: repeated stress runs with `go test ./internal/mcp -count=10` still showed intermittent async timeouts in `TestToolStartJobAcceptsExistingWorkspace` and `TestToolStartChainReturnsChainIDAndStatus`, even though the required verification command `go test ./... -count=1` passed after the Windows state-write fix.

Validated the diff-tracking change already in progress: `internal/domain/types.go` now includes `Step.DiffSummary`, `internal/orchestrator/service.go` populates it only on successful sequential worker completion via `git -C <workspace> diff --stat`, and missing git / non-repo / command failure cases return an empty string without failing the step. `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` documents the new step metadata. Investigated the `internal/mcp` test failure and found an unrelated Windows persistence issue: repeated state writes could fail because `os.Rename` does not replace existing files on Windows. Fixed that in `internal/store/state_store.go`, added an overwrite regression test in `internal/store/state_store_test.go`, and kept the MCP async wait helpers in `internal/mcp/server_test.go` at 15s to reduce timing flake. Commands run: `git status --short`; file inspections with `Get-Content`/`rg`; `go test ./internal/mcp -count=1` (passed); `go test ./... -count=1` (initially failed in `internal/mcp`, later passed after fixes); `go test ./internal/store ./internal/mcp -count=10` (passed once during validation); `go test ./internal/mcp -count=10` (still intermittently flaky under stress); `gofmt -w internal/domain/types.go internal/orchestrator/service.go internal/store/state_store.go internal/store/state_store_test.go internal/mcp/server_test.go`; `go build ./...` (passed); final `go test ./... -count=1` (passed). Non-fatal shell warnings appeared on every command from the blocked PowerShell profile script.
A succeeded implement step covers the job goal and reports the required build/test verification passing.

Sprint contract satisfied. `Step.DiffSummary` was added in `internal/domain/types.go`, successful worker-step completion in `internal/orchestrator/service.go` now captures `git diff --stat` from the workspace with graceful empty-string fallback when git is unavailable or the workspace is not a repo, relevant docs were updated in `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, and required verification passed with `go build ./...` and `go test ./...`. Residual note: `internal/mcp` still shows intermittent timeout flake under repeated stress runs, but the required verification command completed successfully.
